expressions.english: foothold, scaffold, support for the feet
flat feet
footstep
foot odor
footprint
toe
foot massage
to have small feet
to have sore feet, pain in the feet
to have ugly feet
to overwork one's feet
Do these shoes fit your feet well?
expressions.notes:
신발 = shoes
잘 = well; carefully
맞다 = to fit

expressions.english: I think I heard footsteps.
expressions.notes:
발소리 = footstep
들리다 = to be heard, audible; to sound

expressions.english: I walked for a long time, so my feet hurt.
expressions.notes:
오래 = for long, for a long time
걷다 = to walk

expressions.english: Now that I got a foot massage, I feel so refreshed.
expressions.notes:
받다 = to receive
-니까 = since, because
정말 = really; very
개운하다 = to feel refreshed

expressions.korean: 저는 하이힐을 신으면 발이 너무 아파요.
expressions.english: When I wear high heels, my feet hurt badly.
expressions.notes:
하이힐 = high heels
신다 = to wear (footwear/socks)
-(으)면 = if + S + V; when/once + S + V
발 = foot

expressions.korean: 추운 데에 오래 있어서 발이 너무 시려 요.
expressions.english: I stayed in a cold place for a long time, so my feet are really cold.
expressions.notes:
춥다 = to be cold
추운 = the adverb form of 춥다.
곳 = (adjective) + place
시리다 = (a body part) feels cold

expressions.korean: 저는 발이 평발이어서 달리기를 잘 못해요.
expressions.english: I have flat feet, so I'm not good at running.
expressions.notes:
평발 = flat feet
달리기 = running
달리기를 하다 = to run
잘 못하다 = to be not good at + V-ing

expressions.korean: 새로 산 신발 때문에 발에 물집이 생겼어요.
expressions.english: I have a blister on my foot because of my new shoes.
expressions.notes:
새로 = newly; (do something) over
때문에 = because (of), since
물집 = blister
생기다 = to come into being; to be formed; to appear

expressions.korean: 저는 발이 너무 작아서, 맞는 사이즈를 찾기가 어려워요.
expressions.english: My feet are too small, so it's hard to find my size.
expressions.notes:
사이즈 = size
찾다 = to find; to look for
